topic: Polymer glass transition temperature (Tg) experimental measurements
root_query: polymer glass transition temperature Tg DSC experimental SMILES repeat unit dataset prediction

scientific_papers.name: Chen G., Tao L., Li Y. — Predicting Polymers' Glass Transition Temperature by a Chemical Language Processing Model

extraction_strategy.target: Table 1 — 12 benchmark polymers with SMILES and Tg (K)

scientific_papers.notes: Table 1 contains SMILES and Tg for 12 benchmark polymers. Full training dataset of 283 polymers sourced from PoLyInfo — not republished. Code: github.com/figotj/RNN-Tg.

scientific_papers.name: Fatriansyah J.F. et al. — Prediction of Glass Transition Temperature of Polymers Using Simple Machine Learning

extraction_strategy.target: inline Tg values in Section 2; Table 2 contains model comparison metrics only

scientific_papers.notes: 1437-polymer dataset sourced from PoLyInfo — not republished. Data available from authors upon request. Benchmark Tg values extractable from body text.

scientific_papers.name: Uddin M.J., Fan J. — Interpretable Machine Learning Framework to Predict the Glass Transition Temperature of Polymers

extraction_strategy.target: Table 3 — cross-source Tg comparison for 6 benchmark polymers

scientific_papers.notes: 7174-polymer dataset from Liu et al. (KDD 2022) — used but not republished. No supplementary CSV attached. Data available from authors upon request.

scientific_papers.name: Rasulev B. et al. — Machine learning analysis of a large set of homopolymers to predict glass transition temperatures
scientific_papers.access_method: publisher PDF download (CC BY 4.0); Supplementary Data 1 download from Nature page

extraction_strategy.tool: pandas.read_csv() or pandas.read_excel() on Supplementary Data 1
extraction_strategy.target: Supplementary Data 1 — 902 unique homopolymers with SMILES and experimental Tg

scientific_papers.notes: 902 unique homopolymers assembled from public sources, rigorously deduplicated. Data independently collected by authors — not a PoLyInfo copy. Best open structured source for this project.

databases.name: PoLyInfo — NIMS Polymer Database
databases.access_method: manual GUI export only — automated scraping prohibited by Terms of Service

extraction_strategy.target: gap-filling for polymer classes absent from paper sources

databases.notes: Primary authoritative source of experimental Tg. ToS explicitly prohibits automated access. Max ~50 records per export session. Registration required. Copyright © NIMS.

aggregators.name: PubChem PUG-REST API

extraction_strategy.target: polymer name → canonical SMILES — identifier resolution only

aggregators.notes: No Tg property available. Use only to resolve polymer names to canonical SMILES when SMILES is absent in the source.

ml_datasets.name: LamaLab — Curated Glass Transition Temperature for Polymers
ml_datasets.notes: Curated dataset with PSMILES, Tg values, reliability scores, standard deviation, and polymer class. Use for cross-validation only — check provenance column before treating as independent source. Related repo: github.com/lamalab-org/PolyMetriX.
